note: Sunday afternoon edition - the World Cup final kicks off at 3pm.

items.title: World Cup FINAL today, 3pm - where to watch it in Miami
items.blurb: Free county watch parties 2-6pm at Amelia Earhart Park, Tropical Park and Palmetto; big screen at the Miami Beach Bandshell; Grails Wynwood has it on 75+ TVs, no cover.

items.title: Fed week is here: hike odds at 46.5% for July 29
items.blurb: FOMC meets July 28-29 with CPI running 4.2% - hottest since 2023. Warsh has scrapped forward guidance entirely, so the data this week does the talking.

items.title: Marlins swept in Milwaukee - rough restart after the break
items.blurb: 2-1 in ten, then 8-6 twice. Three straight to the NL Central leaders; the wild card cushion needs defending in Houston next.

items.title: Heat and Panthers went big - the Dolphins are preaching patience
items.blurb: Sharp read on the three-franchise split screen: Giannis and Tkachuk arrive while Hafley rebuilds with youth. Heat still monitoring Klay Thompson.

items.title: Week ahead: PMIs, new home sales, and the AI trade gets tested
items.blurb: Alphabet, Intel, Tesla and GE Vernova all report - a real stress test for the AI trade with rates repricing underneath it.

items.title: Dolphins rookies report tomorrow - the Hafley era gets real
items.blurb: Malik Willis (3yr, $67.5M) leads a young roster into Baptist Health. Proctor, Grant and Savaiinaea are the names to watch in camp.

items.title: Philosophy Now: Stoicism in History and Modern Life
items.blurb: A full issue on why the Stoa keeps returning - proper philosophy writing, not listicle Stoicism. Good with Sunday coffee.

items.title: Rubell Museum + the Jon & Vinny's garden residency
items.blurb: 7,700 works of contemporary art, then pizza and soft serve in the garden. A proper Sunday double-bill in Allapattah.

items.title: Why a 2,300-year-old philosophy found a foothold now
items.blurb: The academic take on the Stoic revival - attention as the scarce resource, and the view from above as original mindfulness.

items.title: Miami Takeover returns July 24-27
items.blurb: The 18th annual DC-to-Miami culture weekend - Go-Go, spoken word, art and R&B across the beach. Next weekend's anchor event.

items.title: Peak mango season has taken over the county
items.blurb: Smoothies, salsas, desserts and farmers market hauls - the seasonal excuse to eat well all week.
